title: Character Consistency Engine
description: Develop a system to track and enforce logical, emotionally consistent character behavior across scenes. This includes preserving motivation, detecting contradictions, and optionally validating tone coherence across LLM responses. Future versions may support internal conflict modeling and cross-scene behavioral audits.

title: Image Generation Integration
description: Add optional support for image generation via local or API-driven models. Images are treated as non-critical 'fluff' and stored in each storypack’s `/images/` directory. The system should support pluggable image models, matching how LLM adapters work. Future support may include image memory tagging, automatic character/scene visualization, and CLI-triggered generation.

title: Narrative Heatmap Generator
description: Build a heatmap that visualizes character, location, and emotional 'activity' across the timeline. Useful for writers editing structure and for measuring pacing. Exposed via CLI or exportable CSV.

title: What-If Forking Engine
description: Enable non-destructive timeline forks from any scene. Allow users to explore alternate decisions and outcomes, generating new branches stored in the storypack under `/branches/` with metadata describing the divergence.

title: Emotion Drift Tracker
description: Track a character's emotional state over time and log the arc (e.g., joy → confusion → anger → resolve). Integrate into timeline tools and memory audit logs for post-play analysis and author editing.

title: Character Q&A Mode
description: Enable users to ask characters out-of-world questions using memory and motivations. Characters should respond as if interviewed, using internal beliefs and knowledge, not just improv. This enhances immersion and debugging of character logic.
